questionText: Disclose your estimated total net reserves and resource base (million boe), including the total associated with subsidiaries and equity-accounted entities.
questionKo: Disclose your estimated total net reserves 및 resource base (million boe), including the total associated with subsidiaries 및 equity-accounted entities.
simpleHelp: The transition to a low-carbon economy may affect the value of resources or long-lived assets. Robust data on proved, probable and total resource base is valuable information for data users and investors. Providing insight into organization's reserves and resource base can help to inform investors about the potential impacts of regulatory measures and demand changes on earning capacity.

columns.text: Estimated total net proved + probable reserves (2P) (million BOE)
columns.textKo: Estimated total net proved + probable reserves (2P) (million BOE)

columns.text: Estimated total net proved + probable + possible reserves (3P) (million BOE)
columns.textKo: Estimated total net proved + probable + possible reserves (3P) (million BOE)

columns.text: Estimated net total resource base (million BOE)
columns.textKo: Estimated net total resource base (million BOE)

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Upstream" in response to 1.19.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "Upstream" in response to 1.19.

guidance.text:
General 
Enter your 2P and 3P reserves, as well as your estimated net total resource base, in units of million barrels of oil equivalents (BOE). BOE is a unit of energy based on the approximate energy released by burning one barrel (42 US gallons or 158.9873 liters) of crude oil and is necessarily approximate as various grades of oil have different calorific values. 
Please note that CDP have not asked for proved reserve numbers in this question in recognition that these figures are already rigorously reported in company reports / filings. 
Estimated total net proved + probable reserves (2P) (million BOE) (column 1) 
This is proved reserves plus probable reserves (often referred to as P50). Probable reserves are additional reserves less certain to be recovered, and in sum with proved reserves there is a 50% chance that actual quantities produced will equal or exceed this estimate. 
Estimated total net proved + probable + possible reserves (3P) (million BOE) (column 2) 
This is proved reserves plus probable reserves plus possible reserves. Possible reserves are less certain than probable. There is a 10% chance that actual quantities produced will equal or exceed 3P (hence the term P10). 
Estimated net total resource base (million BOE) (column 3) 
Net total resource base includes the total for reserves, contingent resources and prospective resources.
guidance.textKo: 일반 Enter your 2P 및 3P reserves, as well as your estimated net total resource base, in units of million barrels of oil equivalents (BOE). BOE is a unit of energy based on the approximate energy released by burning one barrel (42 US gallons 또는 158.9873 liters) of crude oil 및 is necessarily approximate as various grades of oil have different calorific values. Please note that CDP have not asked for proved reserve numbers in this 문항 in recognition that these figures are already rigorously 보고 in company reports / filings. Estimated total net proved + probable reserves (2P) (million BOE) (열 1) This is proved reserves plus probable reserves (often referred to as P50). Probable reserves are additional reserves less certain to be recovered, 및 in sum with proved reserves there is a 50% chance that actual quantities produced will equal 또는 exceed this estimate. Estimated total net proved + probable + possible reserves (3P) (million BOE) (열 2) This is proved reserves plus probable reserves plus possible reserves. Possible reserves are less certain than probable. There is a 10% chance that actual quantities produced will equal 또는 exceed 3P (hence the term P10). Estimated net total resource base (million BOE) (열 3) Net total resource base includes the total for reserves, contingent resources 및 prospective resources.

guidance.text: Defining reserves and resources classification: The Oil and Gas Reserves Committee (OGRC) of the Society of Petroleum Engineers (SPE) found in their Comparison of Selected Reserves and Resource Classifications and Associated Definitions report from 2005 that "Most [reserves] classifications recognize three deterministic scenarios with decreasing technical certainty: a low estimate, best estimate and high estimate. While probabilistic assessments are not commonly applied, it is generally accepted that the equivalent estimates on a cumulative probability distribution would be greater than or equal to P90, P50 and P10 respectively. For discovered and commercial volume estimates, the discrete (incremental) volumes within these bounds are generally referred to as proved, probable and possible reserves. The Russian [Ministry of Natural Resources], UNFC [United Nations Framework Classification] and USGS [United States Geological Survey] recognize similar certainty classes but use alternative terminology."
guidance.textKo: Defining reserves 및 resources classification: The Oil 및 Gas Reserves 위원회 (OGRC) 의 Society of Petroleum Engineers (SPE) found in their Comparison of 선택됨 Reserves 및 Resource Classifications 및 Associated Definitions report from 2005 that "Most [reserves] classifications recognize three deterministic scenarios with decreasing technical certainty: a low estimate, best estimate 및 high estimate. While probabilistic assessments are not commonly applied, it is generally accepted that the equivalent estimates on a cumulative probability distribution would be 초과 또는 equal to P90, P50 및 P10 respectively. For discovered 및 commercial volume estimates, the discrete (incremental) volumes within these bounds are generally referred to as proved, probable 및 possible reserves. The Russian [Ministry of Natural Resources], UNFC [United Nations Framework Classification] 및 USGS [United States Geological Survey] recognize similar certainty classes but use alternative terminology."

guidance.text: BOE (or boe): BOE is the symbol for barrel of oil equivalent. The BOE is a unit of energy based on the approximate energy released by burning one barrel (42 US gallons or 158.9873 liters) of crude oil and is necessarily approximate as various grades of oil have different calorific values.
guidance.textKo: BOE (or boe): BOE is the symbol for barrel of oil equivalent. The BOE is a unit of energy based on the approximate energy released by burning one barrel (42 US gallons 또는 158.9873 liters) of crude oil 및 is necessarily approximate as various grades of oil have different calorific values.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of cells displayed.
A maximum of 6/6 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 표시된 칸(셀)의 개수에 비례하여 완성된 칸(셀)마다 점수가 부여됩니다. 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 6/6점입니다.

simpleHelpKo: The transition to a low-carbon economy may affect the value of resources 또는 long-lived 자산. Robust data on proved, probable 및 total resource base is valuable information for 데이터 사용자 및 investors. Providing insight into 조직의 reserves 및 resource base can help to inform investors about the potential impacts of regulatory measures 및 demand changes on earning capacity.
